'''Zeb Taia''' (born 11 October 1984) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for both [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] at international level.

He played for the [[Parramatta Eels]], [[Newcastle Knights]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and the [[Catalans Dragons]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]]. Taia has also played for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]], and earlier in his career he played as a {{rlp|lk|ce}} and {{rlp|pr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>
He also played for Wynnum Manly in the 2021 Intrust QLD Cup.

==Background==
Taia was born in [[Auburn, New South Wales]], Australia. He is of [[Cook Islanders|Cook Islands]] descent as his parents are from the island of [[Penrhyn (atoll)|Penrhyn]] (Tongareva).<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/league/news/article.cfm?c_id=79&objectid=10666182&ref=rss|title=League: Watch out for Taia if Kiwi coach calls|last=Alderson|first=Andrew|date=15 August 2010|work=The New Zealand Herald|access-date=15 August 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121023153031/http://www.nzherald.co.nz/league/news/article.cfm?c_id=79&objectid=10666182&ref=rss|archive-date=23 October 2012|url-status=live|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

==Playing career==
He played his junior football for the Enfield Federals and the [[St Clair Comets]] before being signed by the [[Parramatta Eels]]. He played for the Eels' [[NSW Cup|Premier League]] reserve-grade team in 2006.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com//db/player/t/taia_zeb/index.php]  {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120618105729/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/t/taia_zeb/index.php|date=18 June 2012}}</ref> In Round 5 of the [[2006 NRL season]] he made his [[NRL]] debut for the Eels against the [[Penrith Panthers]].

In 2007, Taia was granted a release from his Eels contract and moved mid-season to the [[Newcastle Knights]]. In 2008, Taia established himself as a permanent first-grader with Newcastle.

In the first week of the finals series of 2011, Taia made a comeback from a career threatening neck injury in which he suffered four rounds into the season.

On 16 May 2012, Taia signed a 3-year deal with French [[Super League]] club, [[Catalans Dragons]] starting in 2013. Taia said about the move, ''"Whilst my wife and I are looking forward to experiencing life in another country, a beautiful one at that, I am forever indebted to the Knights. Not just for the time I have been here but the support they showed me, particularly last season during a potentially career ending injury."''<ref>[http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Taia-signs-with-Catalans-Dragons/55250]  {{Webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20120907071730/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/Taia-signs-with-Catalans-Dragons/55250|date=7 September 2012}}</ref>

On 5 August 2015, Taia signed a 2-year contract with the [[Gold Coast Titans]] to return to the [[National Rugby League]] after three years in France, starting in 2016.<ref>{{cite web|author=Titans.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/taylor-and-taia-to-join-titans-18496/|title=Taylor and Taia to join Titans | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2015-08-05|access-date=2015-11-12|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150925090847/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/taylor-and-taia-to-join-titans-18496/|archive-date=25 September 2015|url-status=live|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

On February 14, 2017, Taia signed for St. Helens in a swap deal taking Joe Greenwood to the [[Gold Coast Titans]].  Taia was part of the St Helens side which won both the 2019 [[League Leaders Shield]] and the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] securing the club's 14th championship.<ref name="theguardian.com">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Salford Red Devils]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref name="theguardian.com"/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

He played in St Helens 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.  It was his final game as a player as he announced his retirement following the game.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==Representative career==
For the [[2010 Anzac Test]], Taia was selected to debut for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] at second-row forward in their loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/444214/zeb-taia-chases-black-jumper-after-sidestepping-blues/|title=Zeb Taia chases black jumper after sidestepping Blues|date=16 July 2009|work=[[Newcastle Herald]]|access-date=13 December 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091011213724/http://www.theherald.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/zeb-taia-chases-black-jumper-after-sidestepping-blues/1569151.aspx|archive-date=11 October 2009|url-status=live|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

On 16 October 2010, Taia represented the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] against a NSW Country representative side.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.cookislandsnews.com/item/75641-from-a-to-zeb/75641-from-a-to-zeb |title=From A to Zeb |publisher=Cook Islands News |date=21 January 2020 |access-date=8 September 2020}}</ref> In the match Taia captained the side. He made his official international debut for the Cook Islands in 2012 in a match against [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]].

Zeb captained 'the Kukis' in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].
